parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff adds ~6600 lines across 84 files including a new retry/backoff system, BigModel error dispatch, and significant client and job-tracking rewrites. Two critical modules—glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed)—have zero corresponding test files in the repo. The deletion of process.mjs and the project's own history of ReferenceError-driven hotfixes (Bundle F) raise real regression risk. The blast radius of auth, retry, and state corruption failures in the untested client layer is too high for a point-release ship.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs has no test file despite 155 lines of changes to auth/retry/error-handling paths
findings.body: The API client module (scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs) was substantially rewritten—155 lines changed—to integrate the new retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs modules. There is no tests/glm-client.test.mjs in the changed-files list. This module sits at the trust boundary: it handles authentication tokens, request construction, retry/backoff decisions, and error classification. A misclassification in bigmodel-errors or a retry bug that double-submits a request would be invisible without integration coverage. The companion modules (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) are individually unit-tested, but the wiring in glm-client.mjs itself is not.
findings.recommendation: Add a tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering at minimum: (1) retry delegation on transient errors, (2) error-classification dispatch, (3) auth-header construction, and (4) behavior when retry budget is exhausted. Block ship on this gap given the module's position on the trust boundary.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) has no test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs manages persistent job state across CLI invocations. It was modified by 78 lines but has no corresponding tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs. Job-tracking bugs can cause silent job loss (user believes a long-running review is in progress but state is corrupted), phantom jobs that can never be cleaned up, or state-file corruption under concurrent writes. The state.mjs layer beneath it is tested, but the tracking semantics on top are not.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: job creation, lookup, completion, cleanup, and at least one concurrent-write or crash-mid-write scenario. Verify that a partially-written state file does not destroy all tracked jobs.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted outright—dangling-import risk given prior ReferenceError history
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was fully deleted (67 lines removed, 0 added). The project's own commit history documents a prior ReferenceError incident (commit 225401a: 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge'). While check-imports.mjs was updated (5 lines), there is no test file that statically validates every import resolves at load time. A single missed import cleanup anywhere in the plugin would produce a runtime ReferenceError that only fires on the affected code path—likely in production, not in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add a static or runtime import-resolution check to CI that attempts require/import of every entry point and module in scripts/ and scripts/lib/. Alternatively, add a smoke test that loads glm-companion.mjs and every sub-module to confirm no ReferenceError. This directly addresses the class of regression the project has already shipped once.

next_steps: Write and land tests/glm-client.test.mjs before tagging v0.4.7—this is the single highest-risk gap.
Write tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs to cover job lifecycle and crash-recovery paths.
Add a CI import-resolution smoke test that loads every .mjs module to catch dangling process.mjs imports.
Re-run the sampling-sweep after the above fixes land to confirm no regressions.
